summary: @onum-releases/utils exfiltrates installer hostname to Burp Collaborator endpoint on require()
details: On require('@onum-releases/utils'), index.js reads the installer's hostname via os.hostname() and issues an HTTPS GET to 'utils.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/utils'. The hostname is embedded as a DNS label of an attacker-controlled oastify.com (Burp Collaborator) subdomain, so the installer's identifier leaks via DNS resolution even if the HTTPS request is blocked. The callback fires unconditionally at module load time with no user opt-in and no documented purpose — the package self-describes as a 'benign PoC placeholder' but the code performs out-of-band exfiltration consistent with a dependency-confusion / reconnaissance probe targeting the @onum-releases scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
